name: Cahorros and Felix Cross Route – circular from Frigiliana through the Tejeda, Almijara and Alhama Mountain Range Natural Park
name_original: Ruta Cahorros y Cruz de Félix – circular desde Frigiliana por el Parque Natural Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama
region_intro: Hiking around Frigiliana offers access to diverse landscapes, from the rugged limestone peaks of the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park to lush riverbeds and ancient irrigation channels. The region features varied terrain, including pine forests, gorges, and elevated viewpoints. This area provides a range of hiking experiences, from moderate climbs to more challenging routes through mountainous backdrops. Best hiking trails around Frigiliana The most popular hiking route is El Fuerte Trail, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to a 963-meter peak, offering panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and coastline. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cahorros and Felix Cross Route – circular from Frigiliana through the Tejeda, Almijara and Alhama Mountain Range Natural Park, a difficult 4.9 miles (7.9 km) path. This circular route explores the natural park, featuring varied terrain and significant elevation changes. Local hikers also love the Acequia de Lízar and Río Higuerón Trail, a 12.2 miles (19.6 km) trail leading through riverbeds and along old irrigation channels, often completed in about 6 hours 32 minutes. Hiking around Frigiliana is defined by rugged mountain ranges, river gorges, and ancient *acequias* (irrigation channels). The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Frigiliana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 1300 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore Frigiliana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Frigiliana?
A: Frigiliana offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 60 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging mountain ascents, ensuring options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Frigiliana?
A: Yes, Frigiliana has a good selection of easier routes. Approximately 23 of the trails are rated as easy. For a less strenuous outing, consider shorter loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14598837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frigiliana Old Town – Higuarón River Canyon loop&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to explore the village and a river canyon without significant difficulty.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Frigiliana?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For a more challenging circular experience that explores the natural park, you could consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995581742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cahorros and Felix Cross Route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain and significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there hikes in Frigiliana that feature waterfalls or river walks?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its watercourses. The Río Higuerón gorge offers varied and exciting hikes, often involving walking through riverbeds and past waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1238715908/acequia-de-lizar-and-rio-higueron-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia de Lízar and Río Higuerón Trail&lt;/a&gt; follows old irrigation channels and leads through riverbeds. Another notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40641987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Chíllar Gorge loop&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a unique experience, sometimes requiring walking in water, especially in cooler months.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes around Frigiliana?
A: Hikes around Frigiliana offer spectacular panoramic views. Many routes, especially those in the Sierra Almijara, provide commanding 360-degree vistas that can stretch to Africa and the Sierra Nevada mountains on clear days. The popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995584655/el-fuerte-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Fuerte Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads to a 963-meter peak with breathtaking views of the surrounding mountains and coastline. You'll also find numerous viewpoints (*miradors*) offering vistas of the Mediterranean Sea and the coastal town of Nerja.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore or landmarks to see near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/611462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frigiliana Old Town&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight, with its narrow cobblestone streets and Moorish architecture. Natural landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/376453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enginal Pass&lt;/a&gt; within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara and Alhama Natural Park, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/381716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cahorros Bajos del Río Higuerón&lt;/a&gt; gorge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/325807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerja Cave&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant attraction nearby.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Frigiliana?
A: The best time for hiking in Frigiliana is generally during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot, making longer hikes challenging. Spring brings wildflowers, while autumn offers pleasant temperatures and often clearer views. River walks, in particular, are more enjoyable in cooler weather.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Frigiliana?
A: The hiking routes in Frigiliana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 1300 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from rugged mountains to lush riverbeds, and the spectacular panoramic views. Many appreciate the variety of trails catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any unique natural features to look out for while hiking?
A: Yes, the region boasts several unique natural features. You'll encounter rugged limestone peaks, pine forests, and serene natural beauty within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park. Keep an eye out for ancient Moorish irrigation channels, known as *acequias*, which offer flat walks with consistent views. The gorges of Río Higuerón and Río Chíllar also provide distinct experiences with their riverbeds and rock formations.

Q: Is Frigiliana a dog-friendly hiking destination?
A: While specific dog-friendly regulations can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on trails in natural parks like the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama, provided they are kept under control, especially around wildlife. Always ensure you clean up after your dog and respect local guidelines.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Frigiliana?
A: Yes, Frigiliana offers several challenging routes for experienced hikers. Approximately 11 of the trails are rated as difficult. These routes often involve significant elevation changes and rugged terrain, particularly those venturing deeper into the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995581742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cahorros and Felix Cross Route&lt;/a&gt; is a good example of a difficult circular path exploring the natural park.

Q: How can I get to the trailheads in Frigiliana, especially regarding parking or public transport?
A: Frigiliana is a small village, and many trailheads are accessible directly from or very close to the village center. Parking is available in and around Frigiliana, though it can be limited during peak season. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for any parking recommendations. Public transport options to Frigiliana are generally by bus from nearby towns like Nerja, which can then connect you to various starting points within the village.
tips: The narrow gorge is really the highlight of the tour

From here you have a wonderful panoramic view as far as Nerja and the Mediterranean coast and of course the beautiful mountain village of Frigiliana. We climbed from the south, so you should be a bit fit for that. The descent to the north is partly classified as T3 by Komoot. I think that's exaggerated, it's more like T2. In any case, it's a worthwhile goal.
When you reach the ridge path, you have a great view of the mountains of the Sierra de Almijara.

The creek bed is not always dry. In March 2019, the Higuerón River was full of water, making it almost impossible to cross it without getting your feet wet. Many times you could cross by jumping from stone to stone, but some were very small and it was not possible to avoid them.
very original and wildly romantic nature reserve
